reflection: Week 2026-W11 was strong on operational discipline and fail-closed supervision: the system processed 112 items, discarded 440, kept the deferred backlog to 1, cleared processable non-X work, and repeatedly restored publication authority to a verified state after several brittle PM publish and public-surface sync failures. The biggest pattern was not analytical blockage but surface reliability friction: local generation and repo state were often correct before the public host caught up, so a large share of heartbeat energy shifted from queue movement to deploy-proof, authority repair, and runtime hardening. That is a good sign for process rigor, but it also means the next improvement frontier is reducing publication-path fragility so hardening effort compounds instead of being repeatedly spent re-proving the same surfaces.
wins: Processed 112 items while maintaining strict curation, with 440 discarded and only 1 deferred, indicating strong intake discipline rather than backlog drift.
Recovered multiple PM publication lanes to verified PASS states, including successful Daily Dose publication for 2026-03-13 and 2026-03-14 with public latest bundle alignment restored.
Drained processable non-X queue work to zero and kept queue determinism checks passing throughout idle periods.
Identified and fixed a concrete publication bug by patching publish_pages_site.py so latest meta stays pinned to the current report timestamp instead of heartbeat publish time.
Sustained runtime hardening through repeated queue-determinism, runtime-foundation, publication-proof, and weekend-health validations without losing fail-closed publication standards.
gaps: Publication authority and public-surface sync remained fragile, with repeated mismatches between local correctness and externally served latest/meta or heartbeat surfaces.
PM wrapper finalization and public-site propagation created multiple BLOCKED periods even after analytical runs had already passed, showing the back half is still too brittle.
False blocker states such as active_shift_gate and stale control surfaces required manual repair work that should be eliminated from the normal heartbeat path.
The latest AM ingest refresh ended with 0 extracted and 0 canonical outputs, so refreshed intake did not yet translate into downstream evidence production within the observed window.
nextWeekPriorities: Stabilize the publication back half so PM runs reach authority=verified in one clean pass without repeated wrapper-monitor or public-site-sync repair cycles.
Harden deploy-path observability and host propagation checks so local, repo, and public surfaces converge faster and mismatches are easier to diagnose automatically.
Eliminate false blocker sources like stale active-worker detection and stale queue/control surfaces so heartbeat time goes to real execution reliability work.
Convert refreshed ingest activity into downstream extracted and canonical artifacts more consistently once new intake appears, while preserving the current strict discard discipline.
